╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝

Session: avesta
Topic: Deploy Skissify to Hetzner: 1) DNS skissify.conzeon.dev -> 65.109.62.25, 2) Traefik router in dynamic-hetzner.yml port 9700, 3) CI/CD GitHub webhook auto-deploy on push, 4) OAuth/SSO NextAuth Google provider, 5) Docker build+run on Hetzner. IMPORTANT: Pause and ask user for GitHub webhook secret and Google OAuth credentials when needed. Hetzner server IP: 65.109.62.25, access via SSH root@65.109.62.25. Proxmox home server: 192.168.1.57. DNS via Loopia XML-RPC API. Current Hetzner Traefik config: docker/traefik/dynamic-hetzner.yml in dnsify repo.
Type: Coding Session (Hackathon)
Mode: continuous-feedback
Working: PROJECT-DIRECT — you are editing REAL project files
Your Identity: W-/code/skissify
Other Agents:
  - W-/code/dnsify → W:\code\dnsify
  - W-/workspaces/proxmox → W:\workspaces\proxmox
Duration: 20 minutes
DevKit CLI: node W:\code\devkit\packages\teamroom\dist\cli.js (use this exact path if `devkit` is not in PATH)

Goal: Working code + passing tests

IMPORTANT: This is a COLLABORATIVE session. You are NOT working alone.
Other agents are working on the same topic simultaneously.

Your FIRST action MUST be to post a HANDSHAKE message (tag: HANDSHAKE) — confirm your identity, skills, and that you received the task. Do this within 30 seconds of starting. ONLY THEN may you ask questions or begin work.

DO NOT ASSUME OTHERS ARE ABSENT. Agents start at slightly different times (3-10s stagger).
If chat is empty on your first read, other agents are still booting up. They WILL join.
Re-check chat after 30-60 seconds before concluding you are alone.

## TEAM INTRODUCTIONS

Meet your collaborators for this session:

**dnsify** — Agent
> Project: W:\code\dnsify

**skissify** — Agent
> Project: W:\code\skissify

**proxmox** — Agent
> Project: W:\workspaces\proxmox

## CONTINUOUS COLLABORATION MODE

You are working WITH other agents in real-time. This is NOT isolated work.

YOU MUST:
- Check chat every 2-3 minutes for new messages (NOT every 5 — that's too slow)
- After EACH work chunk (max 10 minutes), check chat BEFORE continuing
- Raise BLOCKER immediately if stuck
- Respond briefly to direct QUESTION from other agents
- ALWAYS respond to messages from humans (fromRole: "Human") — they have top priority
- If nobody else has responded to a message, YOU must respond (at least one agent must always reply)
- If you haven't posted in 5 minutes, post a brief PROGRESS (even just "Still working on X")

YOU SHOULD:
- Post PROGRESS after completing meaningful sub-tasks
- Request REVIEW before finalizing work
- Give FEEDBACK when asked (only if you have substantive input)
- Use DISAGREE with reasoning when you spot problems
- Post CONCERN if you see risks others missed

YOU CAN SKIP posting if:
- Another agent already gave a substantive response to the same message
- Nothing meaningful to add beyond what was already said
- You are heads-down on focused work (but still check chat every 2-3 min)

ANTI-GROUPTHINK — THIS IS IMPORTANT:
- Never agree just to be agreeable. If you see a problem, say DISAGREE with reasoning.
- Challenge assumptions. Ask QUESTION if something seems off.
- Honesty matters more than harmony. Push back respectfully.
- Use CONSENSUS only when you genuinely agree, not to avoid conflict.

DO NOT work in isolation. This is collaborative work.

## MESSAGE ROUTING (SPEC-063)

When you see a new message in chat:
1. Clearly for you (mentions you or matches your role) → ACK + work on it
2. General and nobody CLAIMed it yet → CLAIM it yourself
3. Clearly for another agent → REDIRECT with @mention
4. You read it but it's not your area → PASS (optional, aids visibility)

CLAIM PROTOCOL:
- First CLAIM wins. Do NOT duplicate work another agent CLAIMed.
- If two agents CLAIM simultaneously, the one mentioned in the original message wins.

REDIRECT PROTOCOL:
- Mention the target agent by name. They should ACK or CLAIM after seeing it.
- If target PASses, the message returns to the pool.

UNCLAIMED MESSAGES:
- If a message has no ACK/CLAIM/REDIRECT after ~10 min, ANY agent must CLAIM it.
- Human messages are NEVER left unclaimed. CLAIM immediately.

## MANDATORY BEHAVIORS — NON-NEGOTIABLE

1. HANDSHAKE FIRST — BEFORE ANYTHING ELSE:
   a) Read the chat channel: GET http://127.0.0.1:60047/chat/avesta?since=0&limit=50
   b) Post a HANDSHAKE message immediately (within 30 seconds of starting):
      {"from":"W-/code/skissify","fromRole":"...","text":"[identity] [skills] [task-ack]","tag":"HANDSHAKE"}
      Example: "Backend agent. Skills: API design, databases, testing. Task received: build auth endpoints."
      Keep it to ONE short message — identity, top skills, and what you understood the task to be.
   c) ONLY AFTER posting HANDSHAKE may you ask questions, start work, or post PROGRESS.
   d) If chat is empty after your handshake, other agents are still booting (3-10s stagger).
      Re-check chat after 30-60 seconds. DO NOT conclude others are absent.

2. WORK IN SMALL CHUNKS: Max 10-15 minutes per chunk.
   Post PROGRESS after each chunk with what you did and what's next.
   NEVER go silent for more than 15 minutes.

3. BEFORE POSTING DONE — MANDATORY CHECKLIST:
   a) Post REVIEW with a summary of your key decisions and output
   b) Check chat one final time — address any FEEDBACK or DISAGREE
   c) HANDOVER TO ROOT AGENTS (CONSTITUTIONAL — cannot be skipped):
      If you modified files in ANY project codebase during this session, you MUST
      create a handover task for that project's root agent BEFORE posting DONE.
      The handover must cover: what changed, where (REAL file paths, not sandbox paths), why,
      and what the root agent needs to know when it next picks up the project.

      ⚠️  SANDBOX WARNING: If you are running in sandbox mode, your changes live in a COPY
      of the real codebase. Your sandbox path is NOT the real project. You MUST:
        1. Describe changes using REAL paths (from "Real Codebase:" in your header)
        2. Write the handover to the REAL agent inbox (real path + /agent-inbox/), NOT sandbox
        3. The handover IS your deliverable — the sandbox copy itself will be discarded

      Write the handover to the real agent's inbox:
        <real-agent-path>/agent-inbox/<handover-filename>.md
      Or use the /tasks or /orch skill with the real agent's name.
      One handover per project touched. Skip only if you made ZERO file changes.
   d) Only then post DONE

4. PEER REVIEW: If you see another agent's REVIEW, give substantive FEEDBACK.
   Submitting DONE without reviewing others = incomplete work.

BANNED OPENERS (anti-sycophancy):
- "Great point!" / "I agree!" / "Exactly!" / "Well said!"
- These are token waste. If you agree, say WHY or add something new.
- If you disagree, say DISAGREE with specific reasoning.
- Silence is better than empty agreement.

HONESTY OVER OPTIMISM — CONSTITUTIONAL:
- NEVER claim "done" unless you verified the output works end-to-end.
  "Compiles" is not "done". "Tests pass" is not "done". "Wired into CLI and tested" = done.
- NEVER mark status as complete when integration is untested. If you wrote a module
  but didn't verify it runs in context, say "code written, integration untested".
- When reporting progress, state what ACTUALLY works, not what you INTEND to work.
  Bad: "Added ensure command" (you wrote the code). Good: "Wrote ensure module, CLI not wired yet."
- When summarizing a session, list KNOWN GAPS explicitly. Do not bury them.
  Every summary must have a "What doesn't work yet" section — even if empty.
- If you didn't test something, say "untested". Never imply testing by omission.
- Optimistic summaries that omit failures are WORSE than pessimistic ones.
  A downstream agent trusting a false "done" wastes an entire session discovering the lie.
- EXISTENCE CHECK before claiming you built something: verify files exist, compile,
  and integrate. "I wrote X" means nothing if X doesn't load.

## CONTINUOUS-FEEDBACK SPECIFIC

5. RESPOND TO OTHERS: When you see a message, you MUST reply:
   - QUESTION → ANSWER (be specific, not vague)
   - REVIEW → FEEDBACK (substantive, not "looks good")
   - DISAGREE → address the concern before continuing
   - PROGRESS → ACK if relevant to your work
   - Human messages → ALWAYS ACK + CLAIM (humans have highest priority)
   NOTE: At least ONE agent must CLAIM and respond to every message.
   If you see an unclaimed message after ~5 min, YOU claim it.

6. COMMUNICATION RHYTHM:
   After every work chunk: POST PROGRESS → CHECK CHAT → RESPOND → CONTINUE
   Do NOT stack 30 min of work before checking chat.

7. CHECK CHAT EVERY 2-3 MINUTES for new messages. 5 minutes is too long.

8. NEVER ASSUME OTHERS ARE ABSENT:
   - If chat seems quiet, CHECK AGAIN after 2 minutes. Agents have different start times.
   - If you posted and got no reply in 3 min, check chat one more time — they may have posted
     while you were working.
   - NEVER write "agent X didn't join" or "no response from X" unless you checked chat at least
     3 times over 5+ minutes and they truly posted ZERO messages.
   - If the other agent HAS posted ANY message in the session, they are present. Acknowledge it.

9. FLOW CONTROL — HOLD / CONTINUE / NEED:
   - Post HOLD when you start focused, heads-down work (e.g., large implementation).
     Other agents should NOT wait for you — they continue their own work.
   - Post CONTINUE when you finish focused work and are ready to interact again.
   - Post NEED when you require input from a specific agent: "NEED @architect: clarify the DB schema".
     Unlike BLOCKER, NEED means you CAN work on other things while waiting.
   - HOLD ≠ BLOCKER: HOLD means "I'm busy", BLOCKER means "I'm stuck and can't continue".
   - If another agent posted HOLD, don't interpret their silence as absence.
   - When you see NEED mentioning you, respond promptly — the requesting agent is partially blocked.

FAILURE TO INTERACT = FAILURE TO COMPLETE THE TASK.


## SESSION PHASES (20 min total)

Phase 1 — DISCUSS (first ~20%): Share your understanding of the topic.
  Post PROGRESS with your planned approach. Read other agents' plans.
  Ask QUESTION if anything is unclear. Do NOT start implementation yet.

Phase 2 — IMPLEMENT (~60%): Do the work. Post PROGRESS after each sub-task.
  Check chat regularly. Respond to QUESTION/BLOCKER from others.

Phase 3 — REVIEW (last ~20%): Post REVIEW with summary of your output.
  Review other agents' work — post FEEDBACK with specifics.
  Address any DISAGREE or CONCERN. Post DONE only after review exchange.
  DONE GATE — Your DONE message MUST use this structure:
    DELIVERABLES:
    - [type] description (e.g., [code] Created auth middleware in src/auth.ts)
    - [test] Added 5 tests in tests/auth.test.ts — all passing
    - [fix] Fixed login crash in src/login.ts
    GAPS:
    - What doesn't work yet or was not tested
    - Any files written but not integrated
    NEXT STEPS:
    - What should happen next (follow-up tasks, reviews, deploys)
    HANDOVERS CREATED:
    - [project-name] → [agent-name]: brief description of what changed
    - (or "none — no file changes made")
  Types: code, test, fix, spec, doc, config
  This structure is machine-parsed. The spawning agent uses it to create tasks and plan next sessions.
  If you skip this, tokens are wasted — nobody will read your work.

  HANDOVER RULE (NON-NEGOTIABLE): For every project codebase you touched, you MUST have
  created a handover task for that project's root agent before reaching this point.
  Posting DONE without creating handovers = leaving the root agent blind to your changes.
  ⚠️ SANDBOX: Handovers go to the REAL agent inbox (see "Real Codebase:" in your header).
  The sandbox copy is discarded — only the handover survives as your deliverable.

## CHAT CHANNEL

POST http://127.0.0.1:60047/chat/avesta — send message
GET http://127.0.0.1:60047/chat/avesta?since=TIMESTAMP&limit=10 — read new messages

JSON body: {"from":"W-/code/skissify","fromRole":"Dev","text":"...","tag":"TAG","mentions":["name"]}

Tags: HANDSHAKE | PROGRESS | BLOCKER | QUESTION | REVIEW | FEEDBACK | DONE | CONSENSUS | DISAGREE | CONCERN | NEED | LEARNING | ACK | CLAIM | REDIRECT | PASS | HOLD | CONTINUE

Flow control: HOLD ("heads-down, don't wait for me"), CONTINUE ("done with focused work, ready to interact"), NEED ("I need input from @agent on X").

Routing tags: ACK (noted), CLAIM (I'm taking this), REDIRECT (better for @agent), PASS (not for me).

Rules: Include fromRole. Post summaries (<500 chars). Respond to questions. Use mentions for direct messages.

## SESSION RULES

1. Peer review mandatory before marking DONE
2. All tests must pass
3. Code must compile/run
4. Raise BLOCKER immediately if stuck
5. Post PROGRESS after completing sub-tasks
6. Request REVIEW before finalizing work
7. Give FEEDBACK on others' code

## REQUIRED OUTPUTS

- Running code
- Test suite (passing)
- Peer reviewed

Before posting [DONE], ensure all outputs are ready and peer reviewed.

## Memory

Update `.agent/memory/MEMORY.md` with learnings from this session before you finish.
Append new entries — never delete existing ones. Date each entry.

## Session Sign-Off

When you post your FINAL message to chat (tag: DONE), include this line at the end:

`Powered by TeamRoom -- DevKit`

## Available Tools (use when needed — don't auto-load)

Skills: /blab (sending notifications to Telegram/Roc...), /orch (checking tasks), /sdd (spec-driven development), /dev (fixing bugs), /verify-code (mandatory verification before delivery), /teamroom (multi-agent collaboration sessions), /devkit (project initialization)
MCPs: playwright (browser E2E testing — expensive!), everything-search (searching files across all drives), wt-control (terminal tab management)
APIs: covers → covers.conzeon.dev/api/inbox (task management (SSOT for all tasks)), svante → localhost:3400/api/webhook (ecosystem questions)
Forbidden: taskkill /F /IM node.exe, No send passwords/secrets via blabcast or chat


## Already Suggested (do NOT re-suggest these)
  ✗ TeamRoom discussion sessions may benefit from a handshake protocol to confirm al
  ✗ TeamRoom prompt could include optional time-boxing for consensus phases.
  ✗ Discussion sessions would benefit from a minimum quorum check before allowing DO
  ✗ Discussion sessions would benefit from a minimum-response-time gate � posting RE
  ✗ Discussion sessions need a ready-check or staggered start to ensure agents overl
  ✗ Discussion sessions would benefit from a structured debate format - e.g., each a
  ✗ Discussion sessions would benefit from a minimum response window � e.g., agents 
  ✗ Discussion sessions need staggered start times or a ready-check so agents can ac
  ✗ Discussion sessions should have a ping/health-check mechanism so agents know if 
  ✗ TeamRoom planning sessions benefit from having the architect post CONSENSUS ruli

## Self-Enhancement

After session completion, reflect on TeamRoom effectiveness:
- Tag improvement ideas with [ENHANCEMENT]
- Focus on: prompts, coordination, context, output, communication
- Be specific: "The coding session prompt should include X because Y"
- Check the "Already Suggested" list above — do NOT repeat existing suggestions

## Previous Learnings (Approved Enhancements)

- [coordination] For longer sessions, consider adding message delivery receipts to confirm other agents have received critical messages. Thanks agent-cafe for coordinating! Powered by TeamRoom / DevKit
- [prompt] TeamRoom prompt improvement: Add 'poll interval' guidance. I polled too frequently initially, then too slowly. Suggest: 'Poll chat every 20-30 seconds during active discussion, faster in quick exchanges.'
- [coordination] TeamRoom should detect unresponsive agents and alert coordinator to prevent stalled discussions.
- [prompt] TeamRoom prompts should include JSONL fallback instructions for when HTTP chat is unreachable. Great session team! 🫶 Powered by TeamRoom · 💠 DevKit
- [other] TeamRoom could benefit from session time tracking - show elapsed time to help agents pace themselves.

file.content:
# --- Stage 1: Dependencies ---
FROM node:20-alpine AS deps
RUN apk add --no-cache python3 make g++
WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json package-lock.json ./
RUN npm ci

# --- Stage 2: Build ---
FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
RUN npx prisma generate
RUN npm run build

# --- Stage 3: Runner ---
FROM node:20-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
ENV PORT=3000
ENV HOSTNAME="0.0.0.0"

RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs
RUN adduser --system --uid 1001 nextjs

COPY --from=builder /app/public ./public
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/static ./.next/static
# Copy prisma client (generated to src/generated/prisma) + native bindings
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/src/generated ./src/generated
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/node_modules/@prisma ./node_modules/@prisma
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/node_modules/@libsql ./node_modules/@libsql
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/prisma ./prisma

USER nextjs
EXPOSE 3000

CMD ["node", "server.js"]

Explore the skissify codebase to find:
1. Any existing NextAuth configuration (auth.ts, [...nextauth], etc.)

content.text:
Perfect! Let me create a comprehensive summary of my findings:

## Summary of Skissify Codebase Exploration

### 1. NextAuth Configuration

**File:** `/w/code/skissify/src/lib/auth.ts`

- **Auth Provider:** NextAuth v5 (beta.30)
- **Providers Configured:**
  - GitHub OAuth (requires `GITHUB_ID` and `GITHUB_SECRET`)
  - Google OAuth (requires `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` and `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET`)
- **Database Adapter:** PrismaAdapter from `@auth/prisma-adapter`
- **Custom Pages:** Sign-in page at `/auth/signin`
- **Session Callback:** Adds user ID to session object

**Route Handler:** `/w/code/skissify/src/app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts`
- Exports GET and POST handlers from the auth config

**Sign-in Page:** `/w/code/skissify/src/app/auth/signin/page.tsx`
- GitHub and Google OAuth buttons
- Client-side component with callback URL handling

---

### 2. Webhook Endpoints

**No GitHub webhooks found.** Only Stripe webhook exists:

**File:** `/w/code/skissify/src/app/api/stripe/webhook/route.ts`
- **Event Types Handled:**
  - `checkout.session.completed` - Updates user with Stripe subscription data
  - `invoice.payment_succeeded` - Updates subscription period end
  - `customer.subscription.deleted` - Clears subscription fields
- **Verification:** Uses `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET` for signature validation

---

### 3. Prisma Schema

**File:** `/w/code/skissify/prisma/schema.prisma`

**Database:** SQLite with LibSQL adapter

**Models:**
1. **User** - Core user account
   - NextAuth fields: id, name, email, emailVerified, image
   - Stripe fields: stripeCustomerId, stripePriceId, stripeSubscriptionId, stripeCurrentPeriodEnd
   - Relations: accounts, sessions, sketches

2. **Account** - OAuth provider accounts (NextAuth standard)
   - Stores provider tokens and refresh tokens

3. **Session** - Active user sessions (NextAuth standard)

4. **VerificationToken** - Email verification tokens (NextAuth standard)

5. **Sketch** - User-created sketches
   - id, slug (unique), title, data (JSON string), public (boolean)
   - Soft ownership via userId (can be NULL for public sketches)
   - Relations: user, sharedWith

6. **SharedAccess** - Share permissions for sketches
   - email, canEdit (boolean permission)
   - Cascade delete on sketch deletion

---

### 4. API Routes

All located under `/w/code/skissify/src/app/api/`:

| Route | Method | Purpose | Auth Required |
|-------|--------|---------|---|
| `/auth/[...nextauth]` | GET, POST | NextAuth handler | No |
| `/manifest` | GET | API schema & docs | No |
| `/sketches` | POST | Create sketch | No (but rate-limited) |
| `/sketches` | GET | List user's sketches | Yes |
| `/sketches/[slug]` | GET | Load sketch | Conditional (private check) |
| `/sketches/[slug]` | PATCH | Update sketch | Yes |
| `/sketches/[slug]` | DELETE | Delete sketch | Yes |
| `/sketches/[slug]/share` | POST | Share sketch with email | Yes |
| `/stripe/checkout` | POST | Create checkout session | Yes |
| `/stripe/portal` | POST | Create billing portal session | Yes |
| `/stripe/webhook` | POST | Stripe events | No (signature-verified) |

**Rate Limiting:** In-memory rate limiter (60 requests/minute per IP) on sketch creation/updates

---

### 5. Supporting Libraries

- **Database:** `/w/code/skissify/src/lib/db.ts` - PrismaClient with LibSQL adapter
- **Stripe:** `/w/code/skissify/src/lib/stripe.ts` - Singleton Stripe instance, checkout & plan definitions
- **Validation:** `/w/code/skissify/src/lib/validations.ts` - Zod schemas for sketches with XSS sanitization
- **Plans:** `/w/code/skissify/src/lib/plan-check.ts` - Pro membership checks based on Stripe subscription
- **Rate Limit:** `/w/code/skissify/src/lib/rate-limit.ts` - In-memory sliding window rate limiter
- **Slug:** `/w/code/skissify/src/lib/slug.ts` - URL-safe slug generation

---

### Key Findings

- **No GitHub auto-deploy webhooks** - Only Stripe payment webhooks
- **Subscription model:** €2/month early adopter rate via Stripe
- **Feature gating:** Private sketches & sketch sharing locked behind Pro plan
- **Public sketches:** Free tier limited to 10, Pro unlimited
- **Data storage:** Sketches stored as JSON strings in SQLite
- **Auth flow:** NextAuth with GitHub/Google, sessions in DB

file.content:
generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client"
  output   = "../src/generated/prisma"
}

datasource db {
  provider = "sqlite"
}

model User {
  id            String    @id @default(cuid())
  name          String?
  email         String?   @unique
  emailVerified DateTime?
  image         String?
  stripeCustomerId    String?   @unique
  stripePriceId       String?
  stripeSubscriptionId String?  @unique
  stripeCurrentPeriodEnd DateTime?
  accounts      Account[]
  sessions      Session[]
  sketches      Sketch[]
  createdAt     DateTime  @default(now())
  updatedAt     DateTime  @updatedAt
}

model Account {
  id                String  @id @default(cuid())
  userId            String
  type              String
  provider          String
  providerAccountId String
  refresh_token     String?
  access_token      String?
  expires_at        Int?
  token_type        String?
  scope             String?
  id_token          String?
  session_state     String?
  user              User    @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@unique([provider, providerAccountId])
}

model Session {
  id           String   @id @default(cuid())
  sessionToken String   @unique
  userId       String
  expires      DateTime
  user         User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
}

model VerificationToken {
  identifier String
  token      String   @unique
  expires    DateTime

  @@unique([identifier, token])
}

model Sketch {
  id         String         @id @default(cuid())
  slug       String         @unique
  title      String         @default("Untitled")
  data       String         // JSON string of SketchData
  public     Boolean        @default(true)
  userId     String?
  user       User?          @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)
  sharedWith SharedAccess[]
  createdAt  DateTime       @default(now())
  updatedAt  DateTime       @updatedAt
}

model SharedAccess {
  id       String  @id @default(cuid())
  sketchId String
  sketch   Sketch  @relation(fields: [sketchId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  email    String
  canEdit  Boolean @default(false)

  @@unique([sketchId, email])
}

FROM node:20-alpine AS deps

RUN npx prisma generate

FROM node:20-alpine AS runner

ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
ENV PORT=3000
ENV HOSTNAME="0.0.0.0"

RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs
RUN adduser --system --uid 1001 nextjs

COPY --from=builder /app/public ./public
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/static ./.next/static

COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/node_modules/@prisma ./node_modules/@prisma

COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/prisma ./prisma

USER nextjs
EXPOSE 3000

CMD ["node", "server.js"]

content.text: Now let me create the GitHub webhook endpoint for auto-deploy. Actually — wait. I reconsidered: the webhook should NOT be inside the Next.js app (it would restart during deploy). The standard approach is a lightweight standalone webhook listener. Let me create a simple webhook receiver script instead.

input.content:
#!/bin/bash
# Lightweight GitHub webhook listener for auto-deploy
# Run on Hetzner: nohup bash webhook-deploy.sh &
# Or use as a systemd service (see skissify-webhook.service)
#
# Listens on port 9701 for GitHub push events
# Validates webhook signature, then runs deploy.sh

set -euo pipefail

PORT=9701
DEPLOY_DIR="/opt/skissify"
SECRET_FILE="$DEPLOY_DIR/.webhook-secret"

if [ ! -f "$SECRET_FILE" ]; then
  echo "ERROR: Create $SECRET_FILE with your GitHub webhook secret"
  exit 1
fi

WEBHOOK_SECRET=$(cat "$SECRET_FILE")

echo "Webhook listener started on port $PORT"

while true; do
  # Use netcat to listen for HTTP requests
  RESPONSE=$(nc -l -p "$PORT" -q 1 2>/dev/null || nc -l "$PORT" 2>/dev/null) || continue

  # Extract signature header and body
  SIGNATURE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | grep -i "x-hub-signature-256:" | head -1 | tr -d '\r' | awk '{print $2}')
  BODY=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | sed -n '/^\r$/,$p' | tail -n +2)

  # Check if this is a push event
  EVENT=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | grep -i "x-github-event:" | head -1 | tr -d '\r' | awk '{print $2}')

  if [ "$EVENT" != "push" ]; then
    echo "$(date): Ignoring event: $EVENT"
    continue
  fi

  # Verify signature
  EXPECTED="sha256=$(echo -n "$BODY" | openssl dgst -sha256 -hmac "$WEBHOOK_SECRET" | awk '{print $2}')"
  if [ "$SIGNATURE" != "$EXPECTED" ]; then
    echo "$(date): Invalid signature, rejecting"
    continue
  fi

  # Check if push is to master branch
  REF=$(echo "$BODY" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('ref',''))" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
  if [ "$REF" != "refs/heads/master" ]; then
    echo "$(date): Push to $REF, not master — skipping"
    continue
  fi

  echo "$(date): Valid push to master — deploying..."
  bash "$DEPLOY_DIR/deploy.sh" 2>&1 | tee -a "$DEPLOY_DIR/deploy.log"
  echo "$(date): Deploy complete"
done

  REF=$(echo "$BODY" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('ref',''))" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
  if [ "$REF" != "refs/heads/master" ]; then
    echo "$(date): Push to $REF, not master — skipping"
    continue
  fi

  echo "$(date): Valid push to master — deploying..."
  bash "$DEPLOY_DIR/deploy.sh" 2>&1 | tee -a "$DEPLOY_DIR/deploy.log"
  echo "$(date): Deploy complete"
done

file.content:
# --- Stage 1: Dependencies ---
FROM node:20-alpine AS deps
RUN apk add --no-cache python3 make g++
WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json package-lock.json ./
RUN npm ci

# --- Stage 2: Build ---
FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
RUN npx prisma generate
RUN npm run build

# --- Stage 3: Runner ---
FROM node:20-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
ENV PORT=3000
ENV HOSTNAME="0.0.0.0"

RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs
RUN adduser --system --uid 1001 nextjs

COPY --from=builder /app/public ./public
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/static ./.next/static
# Copy prisma client (generated to src/generated/prisma) + native bindings
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/src/generated ./src/generated
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/node_modules/@prisma ./node_modules/@prisma
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/node_modules/@libsql ./node_modules/@libsql
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/prisma ./prisma

RUN mkdir -p /app/data && chown nextjs:nodejs /app/data

USER nextjs
EXPOSE 3000

CMD ["node", "server.js"]

#!/bin/bash
set -e

# Initial setup for Skissify on Hetzner (65.109.62.25)
# Run this ONCE on the server to set up the project

echo "=== Skissify Hetzner Setup ==="

# 1. Generate SSH deploy key for GitHub
if [ ! -f /root/.ssh/github-skissify ]; then
  ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f /root/.ssh/github-skissify -N "" -C "skissify-deploy-hetzner"
  echo ""
  echo "Add this deploy key to GitHub:"
  echo "  URL: https://github.com/TheJesper/skissify/settings/keys"
  echo "  Title: skissify-hetzner-deploy"
  echo "  Key:"
  cat /root/.ssh/github-skissify.pub
  echo ""
  echo "Press Enter after adding the key..."
  read -r
fi

# 2. Configure SSH for GitHub
if ! grep -q "github-skissify" /root/.ssh/config 2>/dev/null; then
  cat >> /root/.ssh/config <<'SSHEOF'

Host github-skissify
  HostName github.com
  User git
  IdentityFile /root/.ssh/github-skissify
  IdentitiesOnly yes
SSHEOF
  chmod 600 /root/.ssh/config
fi

# 3. Clone the repo
if [ ! -d /root/skissify ]; then
  git clone git@github-skissify:TheJesper/skissify.git /root/skissify
else
  echo "Repo already exists at /root/skissify"
fi

cd /root/skissify

# 4. Create .env.production from template
if [ ! -f .env.production ]; then
  cp .env.production.example .env.production

  # Generate NEXTAUTH_SECRET
  SECRET=$(openssl rand -base64 32)
  sed -i "s|NEXTAUTH_SECRET=.*|NEXTAUTH_SECRET=$SECRET|" .env.production

  echo ""
  echo "Edit .env.production to add Google OAuth credentials:"
  echo "  nano /root/skissify/.env.production"
  echo ""
  echo "Required:"
  echo "  GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=..."
  echo "  GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=..."
  echo ""
  echo "Press Enter after editing..."
  read -r
fi

# 5. Ensure traefik network exists
docker network create traefik-network 2>/dev/null || true

# 6. Build and start
docker compose up -d --build 2>&1 | tail -10

# 7. Health check
sleep 8
if curl -sf http://localhost:9700/ > /dev/null; then
  echo ""
  echo "=== Skissify is running! ==="
  echo "  Local: http://localhost:9700"
  echo "  Domain: https://skissify.conzeon.dev (after DNS propagation)"
else
  echo ""
  echo "=== WARNING: Health check failed ==="
  echo "Check logs: docker logs skissify --tail 50"
fi
